QF.id: VII Introduction to Securities Broking Operations_46

question.text: STT on sale of equity-oriented mutual fund units settled by delivery on a recognized exchange is:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** STT on sale of units of equity-oriented funds settled by actual delivery is 0.001% (one-tenth of a basis point), payable by the seller. This is significantly lower than the equity delivery STT of 0.1%. **The Trap:** The very low rate of 0.001% applies specifically to equity-oriented MF units. Don't confuse with equity share delivery STT (0.1%) or non-delivery equity STT (0.025%). **Cross-Exam Context:** Series V-A covers STT implications for mutual fund transactions. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** STT on MF units (0.001%) is almost negligible compared to equity shares (0.1%). This makes exchange-traded MF unit transactions cost-effective. But remember: no STT on currency/IR derivatives and debt securities transactions.
